published_prior_art.cite: DornGuo2023 and MastenPoirierRen2025: sharp pointwise marginal-sensitivity/QTE envelope logic
MastenPoirierRen2025 gives sharp QTE/DTE bounds under relaxations including marginal sensitivity; FirpoRidder2019 gives finite-point process-sharpness warnings

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=letter below novelty_target=field; finite-support cell-feasibility is too close to a definitional LP encoding unless strengthened to a nontrivial process theorem, algorithm, inference result, or continuous-support extension.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The one-point claim D_x(T)=H_x(T) fails for finite discrete supports if H_x(T) is an interval hull: with treated support {0,1}, control support {0}, tau=0.5, e=0.5, Gamma=2, D={0,1} but H=[0,1].

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace H_x(T) by the finite pointwise attainable set, or move to continuous conditional supports where interval-valued scalar QTE hulls can be meaningful.
Add a quantitative process-gap functional and prove a sharp lower-bound witness, rather than only an existence claim.
Lift the finite-support LP encoding into a reusable algorithm with complexity bounds and inference-valid sample analogues.

recommended_next_step: Reject v2 and redraft around a corrected pointwise-hull definition or a stronger field-level process result.
